title.plain_text: Postmortem: JWT Token Expiry Race (OPS-355 / OPS-431)

text.content: Postmortem: JWT Token Expiry Race (OPS-355 / OPS-431)

payload.content:
# Postmortem: JWT Token Expiry Race

**Incident:** OPS-355 (symptom) → OPS-431 (root cause)
**Service:** auth-service
**Authors:** Maya Chen (@maya-chen), Jordan Park (@jordan-park)
**Date:** 2026-06-02

## Timeline

| Time (UTC) | Event |
|---|---|
| 2026-05-26 09:14 | OPS-355 opened — intermittent 401s under concurrent load, root cause unknown |
| 2026-05-27 14:20 | #incidents-auth Slack thread opened; on-call begins correlating refresh worker metrics with 401 spikes |
| 2026-05-30 11:42 | OPS-431 opened — JWT token expiry race condition identified; links to OPS-355 |
| 2026-05-31 18:00 | Fix deployed: Redis lock + token_version guard around refresh path |
| 2026-06-01 09:00 | 401 error rate returned to baseline; incident closed |

## Root cause

auth-service had **two independent expiry checks** with no coordination:

- the **background token refresh worker** rotates refresh tokens when `exp - now < refresh_leeway`
- the **request handler** validates bearer tokens on introspection and downstream calls

Under concurrent load, both paths could act on the same session within a ~50–120ms window. The worker invalidated an access token the handler was still accepting, producing **intermittent 401 Unauthorized** responses that cleared on client retry (after refresh).

This is a classic **JWT token expiry race condition** driven by **concurrent token refresh** without a shared lock or monotonic token version.

## Resolution

1. Added a Redis distributed lock keyed on `session_id` around the refresh worker critical section
2. Introduced `token_version` incremented atomically on every refresh; handler rejects stale versions with a structured error
3. Added load test `tests/integration/test_concurrent_refresh_race.py` reproducing the race

## Follow-ups

- [ ] Audit other services for duplicate expiry checks outside auth-service
- [ ] Add `auth_refresh_race_window_ms` metric and alert
- [ ] Document refresh locking contract in `services/auth-service/README.md`
- [ ] Review on-call runbook for auth 401 spikes (link OPS-355 / OPS-431)

## Related tickets

- OPS-355 — symptom report
- OPS-431 — root cause ticket
